questions.text: Why does the Surah begin with vivid images of charging steeds?
questions.answer: To create a powerful contrast: animals show loyalty and effort, while humans often fail in loyalty to Allah.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that energy and courage in worldly pursuits prove gratitude to Allah.
questions.answer: False. It warns that people can be intense in dunya while being ungrateful to their Lord.

questions.text: What does 'kanud' describe about human character?
questions.answer: A person who is ungrateful—dwelling on hardships and forgetting Allah’s blessings.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-'Adiyat teach that people know their own flaws?
questions.answer: It states humans witness their own ingratitude, even if they deny it publicly.

questions.text: What does intense love of wealth do to the heart spiritually?
questions.answer: It can produce greed, distraction from worship, and a cold attitude toward gratitude and the needy.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that loving wealth is always evil.
questions.answer: False. Wealth is a test; the problem is intense, selfish attachment and ingratitude.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about resurrection using the image of graves being scattered?
questions.answer: Bodies will be brought out; death is not the end, and accountability is coming.

questions.text: What does it mean that what is in the breasts will be brought out?
questions.answer: Hidden intentions, secrets, and motives will be exposed clearly.

questions.text: How can a believer purify intentions based on this Surah’s warning?
questions.answer: Make sincere repentance, avoid showing off, and regularly renew intention for Allah in deeds.

questions.text: What is one modern example of being ‘intense’ in love of wealth?
questions.answer: Compromising halal/haram for money, neglecting prayer for work, or obsessing over status and possessions.

questions.text: What is one practical way to fight ingratitude (kanud)?
questions.answer: Daily gratitude: name blessings, thank Allah, and use one blessing in an act of obedience.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests that gratitude is mainly feelings, not actions.
questions.answer: False. Gratitude includes loyalty, obedience, and using blessings responsibly.

questions.text: What does Allah being 'Al-Khabir' add to the Surah’s warning?
questions.answer: Allah is fully aware of inner and outer realities; nothing escapes His knowledge.

questions.text: How does this Surah teach accountability for private life?
questions.answer: It emphasizes intentions will be revealed, so private sincerity matters greatly.

questions.text: How can a believer convert love of wealth into something healthy?
questions.answer: Earn halal, spend in charity, support family obligations, and keep wealth as a tool, not a master.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that Allah will judge only outward actions, not motives.
questions.answer: False. It highlights what is within hearts will be brought out.

questions.text: What is one daily habit inspired by the Surah’s theme of accountability?
questions.answer: Before sleep, repent for one act of ingratitude and plan one act of gratitude for tomorrow.

questions.text: How does the Surah create urgency without mentioning long details?
questions.answer: It uses short, vivid oaths and direct reminders of resurrection and exposure of hearts.

questions.text: What is one misconception corrected by this Surah about ‘good people’?
questions.answer: People can appear strong and active yet still be ungrateful—character is judged by loyalty to Allah.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that being loyal to Allah is less important than being loyal to people.
questions.answer: False. It criticizes humans for lacking loyalty to their Lord.

questions.text: What does 'Al-Adiyat' mean?
questions.answer: The [Steeds] that run / The Coursers.

questions.text: Mention several key images used to describe the charging steeds at the start of the Surah.
questions.answer: They run panting (Dab-ha).; Sparks of fire (Al-Muriyat).; In the morning (Al-Mughirat).; Dust (Naq'a).; In the center / middle of the assembly (Wasat-na).

questions.text: What is the statement Allah makes after these oaths?
questions.answer: Indeed, mankind is ungrateful (Kanud) to his Lord.

questions.text: What is man 'intense' in the love of according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Wealth / Good things (Al-Khayr).

questions.text: Does Allah know the secrets of the hearts only on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: No, He is always aware, but on that Day, the awareness will be manifested to everyone.

questions.text: What is the 'Morning Strike' (Sub-ha) signifying?
questions.answer: The suddenness and clarity of the coming judgment or military victory.

questions.text: What is the main warning of Surah Al-Adiyat?
questions.answer: A warning against materialism and ungratefulness, reminding us of the final accountability of the heart.
